Quote_Text: Him we rightly show is known by none, unless by the Son, and such to whom the Son will reveal him; for the Son reveals him to all to whom the Father would be known, and neither without the good will and pleasure of the Father, nor without the administration of the Son, can any one know God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: And as the dry earth, if it receives not moisture, does not bring forth fruit, so likewise we, being first a dry tree, can never bring forth fruit unto life, without the rain which comes freely from above, that is, the Holy Spirit
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: But the Spirit encompasses man within and without, as always abiding, and never leaves him
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: That virtue which holds the church together is faith, by which the elect of God are saved
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Faith is not to be calumniated, as easy and vulgar, and what every one has. I say, therefore, that faith, whether it is founded on love or on fear, as the adversaries say, is something divine
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: It remains, that by divine grace, and by the word alone, which is from God, we understand that which is unknown
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Such a soul shall never at any time be separated from God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: We shall not fall into corruption, who pass through into incorruption, because he sustains us; for he hath said, and he will do it. And a little after he says, his, that is, Christ's goodness towards them, who through hearing have believed, is immoveable, and turns neither one way nor another
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: But how can that be called good which is not done of purpose? And on this account the world required long periods, until the number of souls which were predestined to fill it should be completed, and then that visible heaven should be folded up like a scroll, and that which is higher should appear, and the souls of the blessed, being restored to their bodies, should be ushered into light; but the souls of the wicked, for their impure actions being surrounded with fiery spirit, should be plunged into the abyss of unquenchable fire, to endure punishments through eternity
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Gill compilation; consult Recognitions for verbatim wording.

Quote_Text: Jeremiah 1:5, 7, Do not say, I am a child; before I formed thee in the belly, I knew thee, etc., his note upon it is, this prophecy intimates unto us, that those who before the foundation of the world are known by God unto faith; that is, are appointed by him to faith, are now babes, because of the will of God lately fulfilled, as we are new-born unto vocation and salvation
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: It is not becoming, that a friend of God, on whom God has predestinated before the foundation of the world, to be put into the high adoption of children, should fall into pleasures or fears, and be unemployed in repressing the passions
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He is the Savior of them that believe; but the Lord of them that believe not
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Clement distinguishes Christ as Savior of believers and Lord of unbelievers.

Quote_Text: But thine order and thy magistracy, and the name of thy court is the church of Christ: thou art his, written in the books of life
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Satan is the angel of wickedness, the artificer of every error, the interpolator of every age; by whom man from the beginning being circumvented, so as to transgress the commands of God, was therefore delivered unto death, hence he has also made the whole kind, or all mankind, which springs from his seed, infected, partaker of his damnation
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Yea, in that body in which he could die through the flesh, he died, not through the church, but verily for the church, by changing body for body, and that which is fleshly for that which is spiritual
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: For what is of God is not so extinguished, as it is overshadowed; for it may be overshadowed, because it is not God; it cannot be extinguished, because it is of God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He distinguishes the issues of things, not substances; for who does not place the judgment of God in a twofold sentence of salvation and punishment? Wherefore all flesh is grass, which is appointed to the fire, and all flesh shall see the salvation of God; which is ordained to salvation
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: By whom is truth found out without God? To whom is God known without Christ? By whom is Christ explored without the Holy Spirit? To whom is the Holy Spirit applied without the mystery of faith?
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: It is one thing to make, and another to create, but both he gives to one; man is the workmanship of the Creator, the same therefore who hath made, hath created in Christ. With respect to substance, he hath made him; with respect to grace, he hath created him
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Upon casting lots the inheritance is distributed to the people of God, and the lot moved, not by chance, but according to what is predestinated by God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Not therefore any thing will be because God knows it to be future, but because it is future it is known by God before it comes to pass
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. God foresees because he decrees, not vice versa.

Quote_Text: All that are loved by God the Father, and preserved in Christ Jesus
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: In Adam, as saith the word, all die, and are condemned in the likeness of Adam's transgression, which the divine word says not so much of some one, as of all mankind—for the curse of Adam is common to all
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Through sin the kingdom is given to death; nor could it reign many, unless it receives the right of reigning from sin; by which seems to be pointed out, that whereas the soul was created free by God, it could reduce itself into bondage through sin
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Because our free will is not sufficient to have a clean heart, but we are in need of God, who creates such an one; therefore it is said by him, who knew how to pray, Create in me a clean heart, O God!
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The sufferings of Christ, indeed, confer life on them that believe, but death on them that believe not
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The church of Christ is strengthened by the grace of him who was crucified for her
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: And to give his life a ransom for many, thus for the many that believed on him
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Whoever dies in his sins, even if he profess to believe in Christ, does not truly believe in him; and even if that which exists without works be called faith, such faith is dead in itself, as we read in the epistle bearing the name of James
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: It seems that the knowledge of God is greater than to be comprehended by human nature, hence are so many mistakes in men concerning God, but by the goodness and love of God to man, and through wondrous and divine grace, the knowledge of God comes to them who were before comprehended in the foreknowledge of God; or, according to the version of Gelenius, who to this were predestinated
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: All these things look this way, that the apostle may prove this; That if either Isaac or Jacob, for their merits, had been chosen to those things which they, being in the flesh sought after, and, by the works of the flesh, had deserved to be justified; then the grace of their merit might belong to the posterity of flesh and blood also, but now, since, their election does not arise from works, but from the purpose of God, from the will of him that calleth
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Those words in Matthew 11:27 manifestly show, that God is known by a certain divine favor or grace, which is infused into the soul, not without God, but by a sort of an afflatus, or inspiration
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: It is the united sense of the whole church, that all the law is indeed spiritual; yet these things which the law breathes out are not known to all, but to them only to whom the grace of the Holy Spirit is given, in the word of wisdom and knowledge
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Our soul is enlightened either with the true light, which shall never be put out, which is Christ; or if it has not in it that light which is eternal, without doubt it is enlightened with a temporal and extinguishable light, by him who transforms himself into an angel of light
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: And they shall never perish: you may hear the undertone, as if He had said to them, Ye shall perish for ever, because ye are not of my sheep. No one shall pluck them out of my hand. Give still greater heed to this: That which my Father gave me is greater than all. What can the wolf do? What can the thief and the robber? They destroy none but those predestined to destruction. But of those sheep of which the apostle says, The Lord knoweth them that are His; and Whom He did foreknow, them He also did predestinate; and whom He did predestinate, them He also called; and whom He called, them He also justified; and whom He justified, them He also glorified;—there is none of such sheep as these that the wolf seizes, or the thief steals, or the robber slays
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Writing to the seven churches, and intimating to each of them their sins and transgressions, he said repent; to whom? but whom he had redeemed with the great price of his blood
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Whatsoever is grateful, is to be ascribed not to man's power, but to God's gift. It is God's, I say, all is God's that we can do; hence we live, hence we excel, etc.
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: A new man, a regenerated person, and one restored to his God, through his grace, says, in the first place, Father, because now he begins to be a son
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Most beloved brethren, we ought to consider and understand not only this, that we call Father which is in heaven, but we add and say, Our Father, that is, of them that believe; of them, who being sanctified by him, and repaired through the birth of spiritual grace, begin to be the children of God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The strength of believers remains immoveable, and that integrity continues stable and strong with those who fear and love God with their whole heart
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He stretched out his hands in his passion and measured the world, that he might at that very time show, that from the rising of sun to the setting of it, a large people, gathered out of all languages and tribes, should come under his wings, and receive the most great and sublime sign in their foreheads
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: To undertake a thing is easy, to fulfill is difficult; for when thou committest thyself to a combat and conflict, the victory lies in the will of God, not in thine own
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: We, who before as blind men, and as shut up in the prison of folly, sat in darkness, ignorant of God and truth, are enlightened by God, who hath adopted us in his covenant, and being delivered from evil bonds, and brought into the light of wisdom, he hath took into the inheritance of the heavenly kingdom
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Virtue is perpetual, without any intermission; nor can it depart from him who has once received it
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: For, if he is said to be glorious in predestination, and predestination was before the foundation of the world, the order must be kept, and before him there will be, a large number of men appointed to glory
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The apostle, in the first place, gives thanks to God for his piety, and signifies that faith in Christ was not a new thing, all, but that this was from eternity prepared and promised by God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: How therefore should he choose us before we were, unless, as he has said, we were before delineated in him? how verily, before men were created, should he predestinate us
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Having life and spiritual blessings prepared, before the world, for us in the word, according to election
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Source_Work: On the Synods of Ariminum and Seleucia
Quote_Text: Not that they laid the foundation of godliness, for it was before them, and always was, yea, even before the foundation of the world, this God before prepared for us in Christ
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: This phrase in us is the same as if it was said, that they may be made one by the power of the Father and of the Son; or without God it is impossible that this can be done
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Source_Work: On the Incarnation of the Word
Quote_Text: God being good, hath imparted his image, our Lord Jesus Christ, to men; and hath made them according to his image and likeness, that they, through such grace, understanding the image, the Word of the Father, might be able, through him, to receive the knowledge of the Father; and so knowing the Creator might live a truly happy and blessed life
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: That phrase, as we are one, referring to John 17:21, means nothing else, than that the grace of the Spirit which the disciples had, might be never-failing and irrevocable
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The devil wrought sin from the beginning in the rational and understanding nature of man; for which reason it is impossible for nature, being rational, and willing, and being under the condemnation of death, to restore itself to liberty
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He only is the true and natural image of the Father; for though we are made again after his image, and are called the glory and image of God, but not because of ourselves; but because that the true image and glory of God, which is the Word of God, dwells in us, who being at last made flesh for us, we have the grace of this vocation
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He received that the gift residing in him, grace might remain firm; for if men only had received, it was possible that it might be taken away again, which is shown in Adam, for what he received he lost; now that this grace might not be taken away, but be kept safe from men, therefore he made this gift his own
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Who will boast that he has a pure heart before God? No, not an infant, though but of one day, the original and law of sin remaining in us
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Christ is appointed a mediator in himself, for the salvation of the church, which is what he means by the house of David, as the subject of redemption
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: This is the constitution of invariable truth, in the beginning of the words of God is truth, that the new man, regenerated in Christ, may henceforth live eternal, according to the image of the eternal God, that is of the heavenly Adam
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: What room is left for boasting in us, when we remember that all things are of God?
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: What is so possible to the power of God, than that he can save through faith? That he can regenerate by it?
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: We do not depend on uncertain and idle hopes, as mariners, who, sometimes sailing rather by wishes than in confidence, the wandering and unstable either drive or leave; but we have the insuperable spirit of faith, through the gift of the only begotten of God, abiding, and leading us by an unalterable course to the quiet haven
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Whom God esteems worthy of honor he calls, and whom he pleases he makes religious
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: That in predestination the church of God always has been; and that the fruitfulness of faith is prepared, whenever the Lord shall command it to break forth, but by the will of the Lord it is reserved for a certain time
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: That the members can cleave to their own head, especially since we are predestinated from the beginning, unto the adoption of the children of God, by Jesus Christ, in himself; which predestination he hath proved, asserting that which from the beginning is before declared, Therefore shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave unto his wife, and they both shall be one flesh, to be the mystery of Christ and the church
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Do not trust in riches; for all such things are left here, faith alone will accompany you. And righteousness indeed will go with you if faith has led the way. Why do riches entice you? Ye were not redeemed with gold and silver, with possessions, or silk garments, from your vain conversation, but with the precious Blood of Christ. He then is rich who is an heir of God, a joint heir with Christ
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Neither can any say, that man can procure more for himself than what is bestowed upon him by a divine gift
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: What is impossible by human desires, that can be possible by divine grace alone, for, who can change nature, but he who hath created nature?
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The grace of the Lord is given, not as from merit of reward, but as of will, according to 1 Corinthians 12:11, as he will, he says, not as is due; wherefore there is no room nor reason for boasting in the creature
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Perseverance, is neither of man that willeth or runneth; for it is not in the power of man, but it is of God that showeth mercy, that thou canst fulfill what thou hast begun
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Source_Ref: ch. 31, §61 (Fathers of the Church, vol. 126, p. 101)
Quote_Text: For all whom God wishes to save are unquestionably saved, and they cannot be saved unless God wishes them to be saved, and each person whom God does not will to be saved is not saved, since our God has done all things that he willed. Therefore, all are saved whom he wishes to be saved, for this salvation is not born of the human will but is supplied by God's good will. Nevertheless, these all men whom God wishes to save include not the entire human race altogether, but rather the totality of those who are to be saved. So the word all is mentioned because the divine kindness saves all kinds from among all men, that is, from every race, status, and age, from every language and every region
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Fulgentius on 1 Timothy 2:4: all men God wills to save = all kinds, not every individual.

Source_Ref: ch. 31, §64 (Fathers of the Church, vol. 126, p. 103)
Quote_Text: These are all those on whom God has mercy because they are preceded by his mercy so that they may believe and be freely saved through faith. The fact that they believe does not take its beginning from the human will, but faith is given to the will itself in accordance with the free generosity of the merciful God
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Source_Work: On the Vocation of the Gentiles
Quote_Text: The people of God hath its own fulness. In the elect and foreknown, distinguished from the generality of all, there is accounted a certain special universality; so that the whole world seems to be delivered from the whole world, and all men to be taken out of all men
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: When John says that Christ died for the sins of the whole world, what he means is that he died for the whole church
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: So Christ was once offered. By whom offered? evidently by Himself. Here he says that He is not Priest only, but Victim also, and what is sacrificed. Was once offered to bear the sins of many. Why of many, and not of all? Because not all believed, For He died indeed for all, that is His part: for that death was a counterbalance against the destruction of all men. But He did not bear the sins of all men, because they were not willing
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: He is not crucified with Christ who is not a member of the body of Christ. When, therefore, our Saviour is said to be crucified for the redemption of the whole world, because of his true assumption of the human nature, yet may he be said to be crucified only for them unto whom his death was profitable
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Source_Work: Answers to the Capitula of Vincentius
Quote_Text: Doubtless the propriety of redemption is theirs from whom the prince of this world is cast out. The death of Christ is not to be so laid out for human-kind, that they also should belong unto his redemption who were not to be regenerated
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: The Lord intercedes for us not by words but by his dying compassion, because he took upon himself the sins which he was unwilling to condemn his elect for
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Not for them alone is the Lord propitiation, to whom John then wrote in the flesh, but also for the whole church which is scattered over the full compass of the world, from the very first among the elect until the last one who will be born at the end of time. Pro totius ergo mundi peccatis interpellat Dominus, quia per totum mundum est Ecclesia, quam suo sanguine comparavit
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: We are they upon whom the ends of the ages have met, having ended their course. We have been predestined by God, before the world was, to arise in the extreme end of the times. And so we are trained by God for the purpose of chastising, and emasculating, the world
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints.

Quote_Text: Learn: before we believed in God, the habitation of our heart was corrupt and weak
Book: Doctrines of Grace in the Early Church (Gill et al.)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Michael Horton, Putting Amazing Back into Grace (appendix); verify primary citation.

Quote_Text: Mankind by Adam fell under death, and the deception of the serpent; we are born sinners…No good thing dwells in us…For neither by nature, nor by human understanding is it possible for me to acquire the knowledge of things so great and so divine, but by the energy of the Divine Spirit…Of ourselves it is impossible to enter the kingdom of God…He has convicted us of the impossibility of our nature to obtain life…Free will has destroyed us; we who were free are become slaves and for our sin are sold…Being pressed down by our sins, we cannot move upward toward God; we are like birds who have wings, but are unable to fly
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix compilation; overlaps Gill/Justin sources.

Quote_Text: The soul cannot rise nor fly, nor be lifted up above the things that are on high, without special grace
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: Our free will…or human nature is not sufficient to seek God in any manner
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: Let us therefore approach Him in holiness of soul, lifting up pure and undefiled hands unto Him, with love towards our gentle and compassionate Father because He made us an elect portion unto Himself…Seeing then that we are the special elect portion of a Holy God, let us do all things that pertain unto holiness…There was given a declaration of blessedness upon them that have been elected by God through Jesus Christ our Lord…Jesus Christ is the hope of the elect
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix compilation on election.

Quote_Text: We are elected to hope, committed by God unto faith, appointed to salvation
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: To the predestined ones before all ages, that is, before the world began, united and elect in a true passion, by the eternal will of the Father
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix; cf. FC664 Ephesians 1:1.

Quote_Text: In all these discourses I have brought all my proofs out of your own holy and prophetic writings, hoping that some of you may be found of the elect number which through the grace that comes from the Lord of Sabaoth, is left or reserved for everlasting salvation
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: God hath completed the number which He before determined with Himself, all those who are written, or ordained unto eternal life…Being predestined indeed according to the love of the Father that we would belong to Him forever
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: Through faith the elect of God are saved. The generation of those who seek God is the elect nation…If every person had known the truth, they would all have leaped into the way, and there would have been no election…You are those who are chosen from among men and as those who are predestined from among men, and in His own time called, faithful, and elect, those who before the foundation of the world are known intimately by God unto faith; that is, are appointed by Him to faith
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix compilation.

Quote_Text: This is therefore the predestination which we faithfully and humbly preach
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: In predestination the Church of God has always existed
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix; cf. Gill/Ambrose On Abraham 2.10.

Quote_Text: Here certainly, there is no place for the vain argument of those who defend the foreknowledge of God against the grace of God, and accordingly maintain that we were elected before the foundation of the world because God foreknew that we would be good, not that He Himself would make us good. This is not the language of Him who said, You did not choose Me, but I chose you
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix on unconditional election.

Quote_Text: He came to save all, all, I say, who through Him are born again unto God, infants, and little ones, and children, and young men, and old men…Jesus is the Savior of them that believe; but the Lord of them that believe not
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix on particular redemption.

Quote_Text: All the sheep which Christ hath sought up by His blood and sufferings are saved…Whosoever shall be found in the blood, and with the mark of Christ shall only escape…He redeemed the believers with the price of His own blood
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: Christ is sacrificed for the salvation of believers…Not all are redeemed, for not all shall be saved, but the remnant…He did not give His life for every man, but for many, that is, for those who would believe
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix compilation.

Quote_Text: Not of ourselves, but of God, is the blessing of our salvation…Man, who was before led captive, is taken out of the power of the possessor, according to the mercy of God the Father, and restoring it, gives salvation to it by the Word; that is, by Christ; that many may experimentally learn that not of himself, but by the gift of God, he receives immortality
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: When He says, No man can come to Me, He breaks the proud liberty of free will; for man can desire nothing, and in vain he endeavors…Where is the proud boasting of free will?…We pray in vain if it is in our own will. Why should men pray for that from the Lord which they have in the power of their own free will?
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix on irresistible grace.

Quote_Text: Faith itself is to be attributed to God…Faith is made a gift. These men, however, attribute faith to free will, so grace is rendered to faith not as a gratuitous gift, but as a debt
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix.

Quote_Text: Of these believers no one perishes, because they were all elected. And they were elected because they were called according to the purpose—the purpose, however, not their own, but God's…To this, indeed, we are not able to deny, that perseverance in good, progressing even to the end, is also a great gift of God
Book: Putting Amazing Back into Grace (Horton, appendix)
Notes: Doctrines of grace LEGEND: (P) Predestination/Unconditional Election, (T) Depravity of Man, (L) Limited Atonement, (I) Irresistible Grace, (Per) Perseverance of the Saints. Horton appendix on perseverance of the saints.
